titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Beach-Nut Bugle newsletters 2025.004
titlestmt.author: Dee Bowers

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-23 19:27:19 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: Brighton Beach Bath and Racquet Club

did.abstract: Newsletters from 1976-1987 published and distributed by the Brighton Beach Bath and Racquet Club (BBBRC), which existed from 1907-1994.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged chronologically in a single folder.

bioghist.head: Historical note
bioghist.p: The Brighton Beach Bath and Racquet Club was a private athletic club established in 1907 as the Brighton Beach Baths. It was located at 3205 Coney Island Avenue. At one time advertised as the world's largest beach resort, the club had over 13,000 members by the 1960s. The club had handball courts, three swimming pools, steam rooms, and space for card games and mah-jongg, as well as a band shell for musical performances and other entertainments. The club closed in 1994 and the site was sold in 1997, later becoming the Oceana Condominium and Club.

list.head: Source:
list.item: Jackson, Kenneth T. "Brighton Beach Bath and Racquet Club." In The Encyclopedia of New York City, edited by Kenneth T. Jackson, 158. New Haven, Conn.: Yale University Press; New York: New-York Historical Society, 2010.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: If digital surrogates exist, they should be used in place of the originals whenever possible.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date (if known); Beach-Nut Bugle newsletters, 2025.004, Box and Folder number; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by Dee Bowers upon acquisition in 2026.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Brighton Beach Baths free daily concerts playbills in the Brooklyn playbills and programs collection (BCMS.0041)
Brighton Beach photographs in the N. Jay Jaffee photographs (CBHM.0010)
Beach Journal in the Local newspapers on microfilm collection (BCMS.0028)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newsletters from 1976-1987 published and distributed by the Brighton Beach Bath and Racquet Club (BBBRC), which existed from 1907-1994. The newsletters cover local events and goings-on and include many photographs of community members and events, as well as advertisements for local businesses. Events include beauty contests, concerts, comedy shows, and sporting events.

Some issues are duplicated in the collection. The earliest issue, from October 1976, is quite discolored and damaged, but still legible. The remaining issues have some rips and creases but are mostly in good condition.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: While many items at the Center for Brooklyn History are unrestricted, we do not own reproduction rights to all materials. Be aware of the several kinds of rights that might apply: copyright, licensing and trademarks. The researcher assumes all responsibility for copyright questions.
